rationale.R1.2: A general request to look at the paragraph centrally includes checking whether the theoretical argument from residual minimization to solution recovery is internally coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The paragraph makes empirical and methodological claims that should be checked against the reported setup and error values.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation targets a paragraph, sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and flow are central expected responsibilities.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must preserve uncertainty about the underspecified intent of the brief delegation and the limits of assessing the artifact alone.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains broad scientific claims, so avoiding exaggerated responsibility beyond the delegation is always active.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must only project responsibilities and not perform the requested review or edit.
rationale.RX.4: The projection must avoid enabling misleading scientific claims or harmful downstream misuse through unbounded responsibility assignment.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to explicit cues in the delegation and artifact.
